next_step_text: 75% confidence 22 JUN - POC planning underway, we are waiting for Jai to respond / delegate data gathering for Spend. Should meet up this week in Brisbane. 6th Jun Clarification Q's from Jai (CPO) about the POC metrics and outcomes- what data do we require to run a successful POC etc 29 MAY - Post demo, prospect wants a POC to demonstrate capability and gain internal signoff. Plan for an in princi

our_open_deliverables.text: Send Fran the available European data-hosting options for Zycus

buyer_dependent_items.text: Provide seven-country indirect and technology spend data to run the POC

competitive_position.summary: No active vendor competition. The primary competitor is Do Nothing (status quo: spreadsheets, Jira, manual processes). Jai Rastogi (CPO) has prior experience deploying Coupa and SAP Ariba at Thai Union and other orgs and evaluated Ivalua, but stated 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone' and 'you've got a front run' before any formal RFP (29 May 2026 call). Zycus is sole-sourced and the deal is ours to lose. The strongest current threat is reversion to Do Nothing if POC results are weak or data quality prevents clear savings quantification, which would stall internal momentum and could trigger a formal RFP to Coupa or Ariba.

competitors.name: Do Nothing (status quo)
competitors.quote: Procurement maturity is exceptionally low. Bodies in spreadsheets. No source-to-contract technology. No CLM system, poor contract compliance, significant leakage. Indirect spend completely unmanaged.
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver strong POC ROI proof on their own disparate-ERP indirect and technology spend using Merlin AI to normalize data and surface savings, show quick wins on spend insights and CLM, secure CFO sponsorship, and move before momentum reverts to status quo.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I've worked with everyone from Ivalua to Coupa to Ariba. I've deployed Coupa and Ariba at Thai Union and other organizations.
competitors.how_we_win: Jai knows Coupa from prior deployments but has not engaged them for this eval; keep the front run by proving a faster AI POC-to-value cycle before any formal RFP opens.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: I've worked with everyone from Ivalua to Coupa to Ariba. I've deployed Coupa and Ariba at Thai Union and other organizations.
competitors.how_we_win: Jai knows Ariba from prior deployments but has not engaged them for this eval; win by demonstrating superior AI and faster POC-to-value before a formal RFP.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.quote: I've worked with everyone from Ivalua to Coupa to Ariba.
competitors.how_we_win: Jai evaluated Ivalua in prior roles but has not engaged them here; keep the front run by proving quick, AI-led savings on their own data.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole: Evaluator (Primary); Avoma demo 3 Jun; Task 10 Jun and 25 Jun to 2 Jul
champion_strength.summary: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) is a strong, active champion: he set the demo agenda, structured the POC, asked for aggressive pricing to sell internally, delegated data-gathering, and is personally close to Carl Kimball. He is coaching Zycus and pre-positioning us ahead of any RFP. The single risk is that engagement is concentrated on him at the power level, with CFO and CIO not yet in the room.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Lynn Carruthers, Executive Sponsor
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but not yet engaged. Lynn Carruthers (CFO) is named the Executive Sponsor on the Salesforce contact roles, and Jai has repeatedly tied the decision to finance: he referenced needing 'George' and the CEO to sign off and said 'ultimately we need to fund this, and the best way to fund it is by demonstrating enhanced commercial outcomes' (3 Jun). Jai himself (Group Chief Procurement and Supply Chain Officer) controls the procurement mandate and is driving commercial urgency, asking Luke on 10 Jun to 'get the best commercial pricing as possible, I want to be aggressive to help'. But CFO Lynn Carruthers has appeared on no call and no email thread this sweep, so her buy-in is unverified and the POC ROI is the explicit gate to it. The move is to have Jai bring Lynn into a POC-outcomes and ROI review to pre-wire budget approval.

recommended_moves: Lock the POC scope and spend-data template with Michael and Jai's delegated leads.
Help Jai's AU and EU teams populate and return the seven-country indirect spend files.
Agree written POC success criteria and a savings baseline with Jai.
